original_hypothesis.title: deployASDRouter.js indicates post-deploy call to nonexistent updateWhitelist on ASDRouter, risking missing critical initialization
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The provided deployASDRouter.js script attempts to call contract.updateWhitelist(addresses.usdcWhitelist, true) on the deployed ASDRouter. However, ASDRouter has no updateWhitelist function in the source. This suggests either (a) the deployment script is stale/wrong, or (b) the intended whitelist management is missing from the router, leading to misconfiguration where the router is not correctly wired to the intended ASDUSDC whitelist contract or not able to manage required settings. In production, this can manifest as router being unable to process legitimate OFT tokens (all refunds), or operators deploying an incorrect binary/ABI mismatch.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is a concrete ops/deployment mismatch signal that can cause production downtime and incorrect security posture (operators may believe whitelist is set when it is not).
deployment_assumptions: Operators use the provided script (or derived automation) to deploy ASDRouter
No other post-deploy step sets required configuration (since ASDRouter has no setters)
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: deployASDRouter.js calls updateWhitelist on ASDRouter; ASDRouter.sol contains no such function; also constructor args mismatch (script passes 4 args; contract expects 5 incl. _asdUSDCAddress)

proof_of_signal.pattern: contractFactory.deploy(addresses.note, lzEndpointEID, addresses.crocSwap, addresses.crocImpact); then contract.updateWhitelist(...)

preconditions: Deployment process uses deployASDRouter.js as written or similar automation

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Deployment scripts do not match on-chain constructor/signature and attempt to call missing method

operational_questions: What is the actual deployed bytecode/ABI used in production for ASDRouter?
How is asdUSDC address intended to be set, given constructor requires it?
Is there a different router version in production with updateWhitelist, or is the script obsolete?
validation_requirements: Reconcile repo versions: check CI/artifacts and tags used for deployment
Compare deployed contract bytecode with this source
Document/runbook for wiring asdUSDC + whitelist settings

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Exploit operational chaos during misdeployment: users send OFTs but router refunds/locks funds due to wrong wiring
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Amplify through repeated failed sends creating stranded balances and incident load
preliminary_attack_methodology.coordination_requirements: relies on operator misconfiguration, not on-chain exploit

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Protocol non-functional; cross-chain minting fails; potential fund trapping if lzCompose reverts

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: Router deployed with unexpected constructor args; asdUSDC address unset/zero; revert patterns in compose
investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Deployment script failures; mismatch between verified source and expected ABI
investigation_priorities.behavioral_patterns: Repeated operator attempts to call non-existent methods

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
